blurb:
This is where we take out-of-town guests when we want to show off Atlanta. The riverside location right on the Chattahoochee is gorgeous, especially if you can get a table on the deck during sunset. It's pricey but worth it for special occasions like anniversaries or celebrating a promotion.

The Southern seafood is what they do best. My wife always gets something with their signature preparation and I usually go with whatever fresh catch they're featuring. The intimate setting makes it feel special without being stuffy.

Just know they do close between lunch and dinner on Sunday, so don't show up at 4:30 expecting to get seated. Make a reservation if you're going on the weekend because everyone else has the same idea about that river view.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM

blurb: This is the perfect solution when your family can't agree on what to eat. It's a food hall with twenty different vendors, so everyone can get what they actually want and still sit together. The kids can get pizza while you grab something healthier, and nobody has to compromise. It's right by the Square, so you can walk around downtown after eating or catch one of the events they always have going on. Good for lunch meetings too since there are quieter spots to sit and talk. Parking can be tricky during busy times, but it's worth circling the block.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
I was skeptical about another pizza place until we tried this one. The thin crust is actually crispy, not soggy, and they use ingredients that taste like they came from Italy. Good for date night or when the kids want pizza but you want something better than the usual chains.

The traditional Italian style really shows in every bite. They source locally when they can but also import from Italy for the authentic stuff. The salads and antipasti are worth ordering too if you want to make it more of a full meal.

The ratings don't lie on this one. Nearly 5000 reviews and still maintaining almost 5 stars says something. Just don't expect New York style pizza because this is a completely different thing.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb: This French bakery makes the best croissants in Marietta, hands down. It's bright and cheerful, and you can smell the baking bread from the parking lot. The quiche is perfect for brunch, and the petit fours are what I bring to every potluck because they look fancy but aren't expensive. Get there early on weekends because the good stuff sells out, especially the baguettes that people grab for dinner parties. It's become our Saturday morning tradition to get coffee and pastries here before running errands. Just know they close early on Sundays, so don't count on them for afternoon treats.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
This is my go-to spot when I'm craving good Indian food without the fancy prices. It's in the Perimeter Center area, nothing too special to look at, but the food is legit and they serve it family-style which works great for sharing. Perfect for a casual dinner with friends or when you want to introduce someone to Indian food.

The biryani is what they're known for and it's the real deal. Big portions, tons of flavor, and they'll adjust the spice level if you ask. The colorful setup might be unfussy but the food definitely isn't boring.

They're open later on weekends which is nice when you want Indian food after 9pm on a Saturday. Just know it can get busy during dinner rush since word has gotten out about this place.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb: If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat, this is a good place to try it without spending a fortune. They bring different cuts of barbecued meat to your table until you wave the white flag, and the cheese bread alone is worth the trip. The feijoada stew is comfort food at its finest if you want something different from the endless parade of meat. Come hungry and wear stretchy pants. It's perfect for celebrating something or when you have teenage boys to feed. Just know that once you sit down, you're committing to at least an hour of serious eating.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is officially on my list of top 5 dinner places in Atlanta and I don't say that lightly. Perfect for girls' night out or when you want to try something different from the usual rotation. The vibe is upscale but not intimidating and the cocktail menu is solid.

Start with the hummus and wings, then go for the chicken shish kabobs with the rice that has lentils and raisins. Everything we tried was a 10/10 experience. The tiramisu is worth saving room for if you can manage it after all that food.

They break between lunch and dinner every day which is kind of a pain if you're hoping for a 3pm meal. But the quality makes up for the timing inconvenience. Just plan accordingly.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb: This little Middle Eastern spot in Franklin Gateway is where we go when we want fresh, healthy food that doesn't cost a fortune. The shawarma is really good and the portions are generous enough to split or have leftovers for tomorrow's lunch. The falafel is crispy outside and fluffy inside, not the dense hockey pucks you get some places. They have good vegetarian options too, which makes it easy when we have friends with different diets coming over. It's casual enough for a weeknight but the food tastes like someone's grandmother made it with care.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb: This is our go-to spot when we need food at weird hours or want a proper breakfast at 2am. It's the classic neon diner right on Cobb Parkway that's been feeding Marietta forever. The portions are huge and the menu is like a phone book with everything from Greek specialties to standard American fare. The breakfast is what they do best. Order the pancakes if you're really hungry or the Greek omelet if you want something different. My kids love coming here after games because they can get breakfast food for dinner and nobody judges you for syrup on your shirt at midnight.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This place absolutely lives up to the hype and I'm not usually one to believe restaurant hype. The atmosphere is vibrant without being loud and the service actually pays attention to your table. Great for a casual dinner with friends or when you want good Mexican food that's not Tex-Mex.

Start with the Esquite because it's packed with flavor and sets the tone for the whole meal. The Pineapple Guava pitcher is refreshing and perfectly balanced between alcohol and sweetness. Everything we've tried has been solid but those two are must-orders.

They're open until midnight most nights which is clutch when you're craving tacos after a late movie or game. With over 8000 reviews and still keeping a 4.9 rating, they're clearly doing something right.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM
